summary: Diff implements backend profile schema extension with bio/joinDate + per-field visibility, adds a migration module, updates profile popover and post/message rendering to use public profile info, introduces clickable/styled @mentions via a resolver hook, and adjusts server/channel ownership checks to use ownerPrincipal. Some requirements are not fully verifiable from the truncated diff, and a few privacy/"safe default" behaviors appear potentially unmet based on shown code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Remove any public display of user Principal IDs across the entire UI (including all lists and user surfaces).
missing_requirements.reason: Some key UI surfaces are not shown in the diff summary (e.g., DM thread list, server/channel rows beyond truncated sections, search/autocomplete results UI). While PostItem/MessageItem/ProfilePopover no longer show Principal IDs, the diff evidence is insufficient to confirm removal across the entire UI.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt (mentions other components but content truncated/not shown)

missing_requirements.requirement: Profile & Settings page: add reveal/hide own Principal ID (hidden by default) plus copy-to-clipboard with visible success confirmation.
missing_requirements.reason: ProfileSettingsPage.tsx shows state for show/hide and imports Eye/EyeOff/Copy, but the diff is truncated before showing the actual UI controls, clipboard call, and explicit success confirmation behavior.

missing_requirements.requirement: Public profile queries must only return fields marked public to non-owners (do not expose non-public fields).
missing_requirements.reason: backend/main.mo shows getUserProfile returning a PublicUserProfile and, for at least displayName, returns the literal string "Private" when the field is not public and caller is not owner. This is still returning a value for a non-public field rather than omitting it or otherwise not exposing it (as required). Full function is truncated, so other fields cannot be verified, but the shown pattern suggests non-public fields may be exposed as placeholders.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated): getUserProfile sets displayName to "Private" when not public

missing_requirements.requirement: Profile & Settings UI: allow editing bio and managing per-field visibility toggles for all supported profile fields (Display name, Avatar, Bio, Join date, etc.).
missing_requirements.reason: State variables and mutation hook for visibility updates are present, but the diff is truncated before confirming that the page actually renders toggles for all fields (including join date) and wires them correctly.

missing_requirements.requirement: Conditional migration should initialize new fields/visibility with safe defaults and populate join date for existing users where appropriate (or a safe/consistent fallback).
missing_requirements.reason: backend/migration.mo initializes bio to "", joinDate to 0, and sets *all* visibility fields to #publicVisibility. Given the build’s privacy-safe intent and the requirement for "safe defaults", defaulting all fields to public appears unsafe, and joinDate is not populated with a meaningful value (it is always 0).
evidence: backend/migration.mo: displayNameVisibility/#publicVisibility, avatarVisibility/#publicVisibility, bioVisibility/#publicVisibility, joinDate=0, joinDateVisibility/#publicVisibility
